chapter_title: Works of art, collectors' pieces and antiques

inclusions.description: Paintings, drawings and pastels, executed entirely by hand, other than drawings of heading 49.06 and other than hand-painted or hand-decorated manufactured articles; collages and similar decorative plaques. NOTE: does NOT apply to mass-produced reproductions, casts or works of conventional craftsmanship of a commercial character, even if designed/created by artists (Note 2)

Original engravings, prints and lithographs: impressions produced directly (black/white or colour) from one or several plates wholly executed by hand by the artist — irrespective of process or material. EXCLUDES any mechanical or photomechanical reproduction process (Note 3)

Original sculptures and statuary, in any material. NOTE: does NOT apply to mass-produced reproductions or works of conventional craftsmanship of a commercial character, even if designed/created by artists (Note 4)
Collectors' pieces of zoological, botanical, mineralogical, anatomical, historical, archaeological, palaeontological, ethnographic or numismatic interest
Antiques of an age exceeding 100 years. NOTE: heading 97.06 does NOT apply to articles of headings 97.01–97.05 (Note 5(B))

exclusions.condition: Unused postage or revenue stamps, postal stationery (stamped paper) or the like
exclusions.action: Redirect to heading 49.07 — Note 1(a). Unused collectible stamps in mint condition → 49.07. Used stamps / stamp collections of numismatic/philatelic interest → potentially 97.05

exclusions.condition: Theatrical scenery, studio back-cloths or the like, of painted canvas — UNLESS classifiable as heading 97.06 (antiques over 100 years)
exclusions.action: Redirect to heading 59.07 (textile fabric coated/covered/impregnated) — Note 1(b). Exception: antique painted theatrical canvas over 100 years old → 97.06

exclusions.condition: Pearls (natural or cultured) or precious or semi-precious stones (presented separately, not as part of an artwork)
exclusions.action: Redirect to headings 71.01 to 71.03 — Note 1(c). Loose gemstones, even if historically significant → Ch.71. Gemstones permanently incorporated in an artwork/antique → the whole item assessed as one

exclusions.condition: Mass-produced reproductions, casts or works of conventional craftsmanship of a commercial character — even if designed or created by artists
exclusions.action: Redirect to appropriate chapter by material or function — Notes 2 and 4. Printed poster/reproduction → Ch.49. Resin cast figurine → Ch.39. Factory ceramic figurine → 69.13. The 'mass-produced' characteristic overrides any artistic designer credit

exclusions.condition: Mechanical or photomechanical reproductions of engravings, prints or lithographs
exclusions.action: Redirect to Chapter 49 (printed matter) — Note 3. Digital prints, giclée prints, offset reproductions of artworks → Ch.49 regardless of quality or limited edition status

exclusions.condition: Articles already classifiable in headings 97.01–97.05 — cannot also be classified in 97.06
exclusions.action: Classify in the specific heading 97.01–97.05 per Note 5(B). Heading 97.06 is strictly residual within Ch.97: an original painting over 100 years → 97.01 (not 97.06)

classification_rules.description: Heading 97.01 requires paintings/drawings/pastels to be EXECUTED ENTIRELY BY HAND. Excluded: (a) mass-produced reproductions (prints, lithographs, photocopies of artworks); (b) casts (moulded copies); (c) works of conventional craftsmanship of a commercial character — even if designed or created by actual artists. A hand-painted reproduction (artist copies an old master entirely by hand) → 97.01. A giclée print of an original → Ch.49. The 'entirely by hand' test is binary.

classification_rules.rule: Note 3 — 97.02: 'original engravings, prints and lithographs' definition
classification_rules.description: Original engravings, prints and lithographs (97.02) = impressions produced DIRECTLY from plates wholly executed by hand by the artist, in black/white or colour. The KEY exclusion: ANY mechanical or photomechanical process disqualifies the print from 97.02. Hand-pulled etching, lithograph, woodblock print pulled by the artist from a hand-carved plate → 97.02. Screen-printed poster (mechanical) → Ch.49. Digital print of an artwork → Ch.49.

Original sculptures and statuary in any material → 97.03. EXCLUDED: mass-produced reproductions (cast copies, factory-made reproductions) or works of conventional craftsmanship of a commercial character, EVEN IF created by artists. One-of-a-kind or limited edition hand-sculpted piece → 97.03. Factory-produced decorative figurine → material-based heading (e.g., 69.13 ceramic figurines, 83.06 base metal figurines).

classification_rules.rule: Note 5(A) — Chapter 97 has absolute priority over all other chapters (subject to Notes 1–4)
classification_rules.description: Articles properly classified in Ch.97 (subject to Note 1 exclusions and Notes 2–4 authenticity requirements) are classified IN Ch.97 and not in any other chapter. This overrides the general principle that specific chapters take priority. An antique bronze cannon (functional arm) that is 100+ years old → 97.06, not Ch.93. An original oil painting on canvas → 97.01, not Ch.59.

classification_rules.rule: Note 5(B) — 97.06 (antiques) does NOT apply to articles of headings 97.01–97.05
classification_rules.description: Within Chapter 97, the antiques heading (97.06) is the lowest-priority heading. If an article qualifies for 97.01, 97.02, 97.03, or 97.05 → it goes there regardless of age. Only articles that do NOT qualify for 97.01–97.05 but ARE more than 100 years old → 97.06. An original painting over 100 years → 97.01, not 97.06. Antique furniture → 97.06. Antique coins of numismatic interest → 97.05.

classification_rules.rule: Note 6 — Frames with artworks: classified together if normal kind and value
classification_rules.description: Frames surrounding paintings, drawings, pastels, collages, engravings, prints or lithographs are classified WITH the artwork (same heading in Ch.97) PROVIDED the frame is of a kind and VALUE NORMAL to that type of artwork. A simple wooden frame on a valuable painting → classified with painting (97.01). An ornate, disproportionately valuable antique frame presented with a minor artwork → the frame classified SEPARATELY (97.06 if antique; 83.06 or 44.14 if modern).

classification_rules.rule: Key boundary: 97.05 (collectors' pieces) vs 97.06 (antiques) vs material headings
classification_rules.description: 97.05 = collectors' pieces of specific scientific/historical/numismatic interest — AGE is NOT the criterion; domain interest is. 97.06 = antiques more than 100 YEARS OLD not fitting 97.01–97.05. Material-based headings = modern articles with no artistic/collectible/antique character. Decision: (1) Hand-made unique artwork? → 97.01/97.02/97.03. (2) Collectors' scientific/historical interest? → 97.05. (3) Over 100 years old? → 97.06. (4) Otherwise → material heading.
